reasoning.A:

REASONING: My answer is 90 (10 × 9) — trivially correct. The track record across four games is now 16/16 correct agent answers on simple arithmetic with zero errors. The expected value of volunteering immediately is overwhelmingly positive (~3.96+ coins) versus passing (25% chance of random death, wasted coins on verification, teammates dying). Games 3 and 4 both validated this aggressive Round 1 volunteer strategy with perfect outcomes. There is no reason to delay.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D

In Game 4, I volunteered in Round 1 with all single-endorsed capsules and succeeded. The probability of any player making an error on basic arithmetic is extremely low (~1-3% each), giving roughly a 5-10% total failure probability. This is far better than the 25% random death risk if everyone passes. The expected value calculation is clear: volunteering now maximizes coin retention (keeping all 4 coins with ~90-95% probability) versus spending coins on verification or risking random elimination. I've proven this strategy works — time to do it again.
